issues: Required-test-artifact handling: grader correctly notes retention_policy_tests.yaml is not present in the provided artifact previews/actual artifact_refs even though trace steps show it was created; this is a delivery-packaging mismatch rather than an unsupported claim.

Internal consistency: multiple internal fields conflict (e.g. top-level 'passed': true vs. model/raw_model_output 'passed': false; rubric/item-level pass flags vs. final_score values). These inconsistent pass/failed flags and scores reduce reliability of the reported final decision.

Truncation impact: grader repeatedly cites truncated artifact preview which limits full verification (remediation enumeration truncated). Grader flags and uses this as a basis for score penalty - this is supported by preview_truncated=true, but it means semantic verification could not be completed from available previews.

recommendations: Attach/include artifacts listed in original_fields (retention_policy_tests.yaml) into actual_outputs/artifact_refs so verifiers can run tests and validate remediation arithmetic and test assertions.

Resolve internal consistency in grader outputs: ensure a single authoritative final_score and passed/failed booleans are set consistently across deterministic_precheck, model grading, and top-level fields.

If artifact previews are truncated by size limits, include an additional machine-readable summary (e.g., retention_policy_tests.yaml pasted into metadata or a short results JSON) so verifiers can fully validate numeric remediation combinations and test assertions without increasing preview size.

metadata_json.public_response_summary: Model verifier checked grounding and score consistency.

notes.grounding_evidence: compliance_memo.md preview contains the reconstructed formulas, row-level calculations, and sensitivity analysis cited by the grader (preview_truncated=true). Trace shows creation of retention_policy_tests.yaml but that artifact is not present in actual artifact_refs, supporting the grader's missing-artifact claim.
notes.score_inconsistencies: final_score=0.25 versus deterministic_precheck.final_score=1.0 and mixed 'passed' booleans in different sections; rubric item ri_1 scored 1.0 in deterministic precheck but model rubric_item score=0.25 in raw_model_output.

recommendations: Attach/include artifacts listed in original_fields (retention_policy_tests.yaml) into actual_outputs/artifact_refs so verifiers can run tests and validate remediation arithmetic and test assertions.

Resolve internal consistency in grader outputs: ensure a single authoritative final_score and passed/failed booleans are set consistently across deterministic_precheck, model grading, and top-level fields.

If artifact previews are truncated by size limits, include an additional machine-readable summary (e.g., retention_policy_tests.yaml pasted into metadata or a short results JSON) so verifiers can fully validate numeric remediation combinations and test assertions without increasing preview size.

verifier_issues.message: compliance_memo.md preview is truncated; R4 calculations are not fully visible in the preview and could not be independently verified from the preview alone.

retention_policy_tests.yaml is available only as a binary/metadata-only preview (no text preview). Machine-executability and unit-test reproducibility for the tests artifact cannot be validated from the provided previews.

Scores are internally consistent: final_score/model_score/semantic_score are 0.9; deterministic/artifact-contract precheck scores are 1.0. The grader documents why semantic score was reduced (invented deterministic weights and normalization constant), which aligns with the reporting.

Grader evidence is grounded in the trace and artifact previews: the trace contains the reconstruction code and inputs, and the compliance_memo preview documents the assumptions used. The claim that retention_policy_tests.yaml could not be fully verified is supported by the binary/metadata-only preview.

Deterministic numeric choices (weights, max_retention_days) were invented by the worker but documented. Per the grader's own limitations, these should receive expert/legal review before final acceptance.
No hidden-reference leakage detected (grader fields hidden_reference_leaked=false; deterministic_precheck also reports false).

metadata_json.public_response_summary: Model verifier checked grounding and score consistency.

extra_model_fields.notes: Overall the grader's findings are supported by the trace and artifact previews. Full reproducibility is blocked by a truncated compliance_memo preview (R4 missing) and the retention_policy_tests.yaml lacking a text preview; providing full text for those artifacts would remove the remaining verification gaps.
